task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI main window is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The GUI is playing back EEG data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI indicates it is in playback mode (e.g., 'PLAYBACK' system control panel is active or data is actively scrolling) rather than LIVE or SYNTHETIC.

task_completion.description: The Time Series widget is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget labeled 'Time Series' showing scrolling EEG waveforms is visible in the GUI dashboard.

task_completion.description: The Head Plot widget is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget labeled 'Head Plot' showing a topographic map of a head is visible in the GUI dashboard alongside the Time Series widget.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the session is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, screenshot tool, file manager, or terminal command showing the image being saved specifically to '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/head_plot_session.png'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine interaction with the OpenBCI GUI interface (e.g., clicking dropdowns, selecting widgets) rather than just viewing an image or text file.
The Time Series and Head Plot widgets show complex, realistic EEG data patterns, not empty grids or static placeholder text.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass GUI configuration by copying a pre-existing screenshot.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory must show the GUI being configured before the screenshot is taken, rather than a single terminal command copying a file to the destination.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/head_plot_session.png' with a minimum file size of 50KB. The OpenBCI GUI must be configured to show both 'Head Plot' and 'Time Series' widgets simultaneously. The recording file used for playback should be 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'.
